PRE_CLAUDE_MISSING: Claude CLI not found — bridge will not work!
PRE_CLAUDE_CONT: (Continuing setup — install Claude CLI before starting services)
AUTH_NEEDED: Claude CLI — login required. Starting 'claude auth login'...
AUTH_APIKEY: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set — API key auth will be used (no login needed)
AUTH_INSTR: A browser window will open (or follow the URL shown below). Log in with your Claude account.
AUTH_WARN: Authentication may be incomplete — run 'claude auth login' manually if Bridge fails to start
AUTH_SKIP: Claude CLI not found — skipping auth step
AUTH_INSTALLING: Claude CLI not found — installing via npm (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code)...
AUTH_INSTALL_FAIL: Claude CLI install failed. Install manually: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
AUTH_NPM_MISSING: Claude CLI missing AND npm missing. Install Node.js 18+ first: https://nodejs.org then re-run install.sh
AUTH_WINGET_INSTALLING: npm not found — installing Node.js LTS via winget (this may take a few minutes)...
AUTH_CHOCO_INSTALLING: npm not found (winget failed or unavailable) — installing Node.js LTS via Chocolatey (this may take a few minutes)...
STEP_PKG_ALL: ↳ No capability config found — installing all packages
STEP_DOCKER_ADDED: Docker: user added to 'docker' group (re-login to activate)
STEP_DOCKER_WARN: Docker installed but user is not in 'docker' group.
STEP_CF_FAIL: cloudflared installation failed — install manually before starting services
STEP_SYSTEMD_MISSING: systemd not found, skipping unit files
STEP_SYSTEMD_START: To start: sudo systemctl start personal-agent personal-agent-bridge
STEP_SYSTEMD_NOROOT: No root access; unit files created at
STEP_PM2_STARTUP: pm2 startup may require root — run the printed command
WIZ_WELCOME_MSG:
Welcome! This wizard will configure your .env file step by step.

Press Enter to confirm each step, ESC to cancel.

Press OK to begin.
WIZ_ENV_EXISTS_MSG: .env appears to be already filled.\nRun the wizard again?
WIZ_ENV_SKIP_CI: Non-interactive terminal — .env wizard skipped. Fill in manually:
WIZ_LLM_MSG: Which AI model do you want to use?
WIZ_PRX_MSG:
How will Meta/Telegram reach your server?
(The webhook endpoint must be publicly accessible)
WIZ_PRX_NONE: None — Local dev / VPS with static IP
WIZ_PRX_NGROK: ngrok — Free static domain  ✅ recommended for most users
WIZ_PRX_EXT: External URL — You have your own domain
WIZ_WA_INFO_MSG:
Get credentials from Meta Developer Console:
  developers.facebook.com → My Apps → <Your App> → WhatsApp → API Setup

  Access Token   — temporary 24h token on that page, or create a permanent
                   System User token in Business Settings → System Users
  Phone Number ID — numeric ID shown next to your phone number (e.g. 1234567890)
  App Secret     — Settings → Basic → App Secret  (used to verify webhook signatures)
  Owner Number   — your own WhatsApp number WITH country code (e.g. +1XXXXXXXXXX)

  Verify Token and webhook secret are auto-generated — no input needed.

Press OK when ready.
WIZ_TG_INFO_MSG:
You will enter 2 values on the next screens:
  1. Bot Token  — get it from @BotFather
  2. Chat ID    — auto-detected (just send a message to your bot)

How to get the Bot Token:
  1. Open Telegram → search @BotFather → /newbot
  2. Choose a name and username (must end with 'bot')
  3. BotFather gives you a token like: 123456789:ABCdef...

Press OK to enter the Bot Token.
WIZ_TG_SEND_MSG:
Open your new Telegram bot and send any message (e.g. 'hello').

  How to find your bot:
  • In Telegram, search for the username you just set with BotFather
  • Tap START or send any text

  Click OK — the wizard will then wait for your message and detect your Chat ID automatically (90 seconds).
  Press Enter to type it manually.
TXT_TG_CHATID_TIP: Open your bot in Telegram, send 'hello', then press Enter to auto-detect your Chat ID (or type it manually):
TXT_TG_TEST_MSG: ✅ Bot connected. Now enter your ngrok credentials in the terminal — we'll continue here once you're done.
TXT_TG_TEST_MSG_READY:
🚀 Ready! Public URL: %s

Remaining questions will be sent here as buttons.
MSG_TG_BACK_TO_TERMINAL: 💻 Switch back to the install machine — credentials are entered in the terminal.
CAP_TG_INTRO:
🔧 *Capability Modules*

Accept defaults or choose one by one.
TXT_TG_TIMEOUT: No response received — continuing with default
TXT_TG_CHATID_FAIL:
Auto-detect failed. Get your Chat ID in 10 seconds:
  → Open Telegram → search @userinfobot → tap Start → it replies with your ID
  → Copy the number (e.g. 123456789) and enter it below.
NGROK_TOKEN_INVALID: ngrok auth token must be alphanumeric and at least 16 characters.
NGROK_DOMAIN_INVALID: ngrok domain must contain a dot (e.g. yourname.ngrok-free.app).
WIZ_AN_CHOICE_MSG:
How do you want to authenticate with Anthropic?

  [1] Claude Login  ✦ RECOMMENDED
      Sign in with your claude.ai account (Pro/Max subscription).
      More secure — no key to manage or leak. The wizard runs
      'claude auth login' automatically after this step.

  [2] API Key
      Pay-per-use key from Anthropic Console.
      console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys → Create Key
WIZ_AN_SKIP: Claude Login selected — 'claude auth login' will run after wizard
WIZ_OL_INFO_MSG:
Ollama must be running locally before starting the agent.

  Install:     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
  Pull model:  ollama pull llama3   (or mistral, qwen2, gemma3, phi3 …)
  Check:       curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags

  Tip: basic conversation works well; complex tool use may be unreliable.
WIZ_GE_INFO_MSG:
Get your free API key from Google AI Studio:
  aistudio.google.com → Get API Key → Create API key

  Default model: gemini-2.0-flash (fast, good for most tasks).
  Use gemini-1.5-pro for complex reasoning.
WIZ_CF_INFO_MSG:
Cloudflare Tunnel gives a persistent free HTTPS URL.

  Install cloudflared:
    curl -L https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/releases/latest/download/cloudflared-linux-amd64 \
         -o /usr/local/bin/cloudflared && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cloudflared

  Create a persistent tunnel (one-time setup):
    cloudflared tunnel login
    cloudflared tunnel create personal-agent
    cloudflared tunnel route dns personal-agent <your-subdomain>

  Or use a quick temporary tunnel (no account needed):
    cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:8010

  (Can be done after setup)
WIZ_CF_MISSING: cloudflared binary not found — install it before starting services.
WIZ_NGROK_INFO_MSG:
ngrok creates a public HTTPS tunnel to your local server.
  No binary installation needed — the agent manages ngrok via the pyngrok package.

  Free accounts include ONE permanent static domain (URL never changes):
    ngrok Dashboard → Domains → New Domain → claim your free domain

  To get your auth token (required for static domain):
    ngrok.com → Sign up (free) → Dashboard → Your Authtoken

  Leave the token blank to use ngrok anonymously — URL changes on every restart.
WIZ_NGROK_TOKEN:
ngrok Auth Token — alphanumeric string from Dashboard → Your Authtoken
  (NOT the domain name; leave blank for anonymous mode):
WIZ_NGROK_DOMAIN:
Static domain from ngrok Dashboard → Domains
  (e.g. yourname.ngrok-free.app — leave blank if you have no account):
WIZ_SEC_MSG:
All security keys have been generated automatically.
No input needed — they are written to .env.

What they are used for:
  API_KEY     — authenticates internal /agent/* API calls
  TOTP_SECRET — 6-digit code for all protected commands

You will scan the QR code on the next screen to add it to Google Authenticator.
Store the secret in a password manager as backup.
WIZ_TZ_MSG: Select the timezone for APScheduler and cron expressions:
TXT_HINT: Leave blank to accept [default] values shown in brackets.
TXT_MESSENGER: ▶ Messenger Platform  (which app you will use to control the agent)
TXT_M1: 1) whatsapp  — Meta Cloud API   [requires Meta app + verified business]
TXT_M2: 2) telegram  — Telegram Bot   ✅ RECOMMENDED — easiest setup, no business account
TXT_M3: 3) cli       — Terminal only   [for local testing, no app needed]
TXT_LLM: ▶ LLM Backend  (which AI model powers the agent)
TXT_L1: 1) anthropic — Anthropic Claude  [recommended — full tool use, best results] (default)
TXT_L2: 2) ollama    — Local Ollama      [free, fully local, limited tool support]
TXT_L3: 3) gemini    — Google Gemini     [free quota, cloud, works for most tasks]
TXT_PROXY: ▶ Webhook Proxy  (how Meta/Telegram delivers messages to your server)
TXT_P1: 1) none        — No proxy   [only if server already has a public IP/domain]
TXT_P2: 2) ngrok       — ngrok tunnel  ✅ RECOMMENDED — free static domain, easiest
TXT_P3: 3) cloudflared — Cloudflare Tunnel  [persistent free URL; requires Cloudflare account]
TXT_P4: 4) external    — Your own domain  [enter your public HTTPS URL manually]
TXT_WA: ▶ WhatsApp Credentials  (from developers.facebook.com → WhatsApp → API Setup)
TXT_TG: ▶ Telegram Credentials  (create a bot at t.me/BotFather)
TXT_AN: ▶ Anthropic API Key  (console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys)
TXT_SEC_DONE: Security keys generated automatically (API key, TOTP secrets)
TOTP_GA_STEPS:
1. Open Google Authenticator (or any TOTP app)
  2. Tap '+' → 'Scan QR code'
  3. Scan the QR code above
  4. Done — use the 6-digit code when prompted
TOTP_GA_NOQUR: No QR code? Enter the secret manually in your TOTP app.
TOTP_QR_ONLINE: Open in browser to view the QR code:
TXT_NOWHIPTAIL: whiptail not found or terminal not compatible — using text mode.
TOTP_SUBTITLE: Scan QR code or enter secret manually
TOTP_QR_HINT: (For QR code: sudo apt install qrencode)
TOTP_WARN: Store this secret somewhere safe — it won't be shown again.
TOTP_NO_QR_HEADING: QR code could not be generated. Note the secret below.
TOTP_NO_QR_SEND_HINT: Press 1 to send secrets via messenger, or Enter to skip:
TOTP_NO_QR_SENT: Secrets sent. You can close the terminal.
TOTP_NO_QR_SEND_FAIL: Could not send — note the secrets above and keep them safe. You can close the terminal.
TOTP_NO_QR_CLOSE: Secrets noted above. You can close the terminal.
WH_WA_CONSOLE: Register this URL in Meta Developer Console:
WH_WA_PROXY_WARN: Local IP won't work for webhooks. Set up ngrok/cloudflared/external proxy.
WH_TG_NO_URL: A Public URL is required. Set PUBLIC_URL in .env, then:
WH_TG_PROXY_RUNTIME: Webhook will be auto-registered when services start (proxy URL assigned at runtime)
WH_TG_REGISTER_HINT: To trigger manually if needed: bash install.sh --register-webhook
RW_NO_ENV: .env not found — complete the installation first
RW_NOT_TG: This command is only applicable for Telegram
RW_STATIC_PROXY: Static public URL detected — webhook should already be registered
RW_WAITING: Waiting for service public URL (up to 5 min)...
RW_TIMEOUT: Timed out — make sure the service is running and try again
RW_HINT_CMD: To auto-register the webhook once services are ready:
WH_CLI: CLI mode — no webhook setup needed.
WH_CLI_HINT: Start FastAPI and test from terminal.
DOCKER_BUILD: Building Docker image with selected capabilities →
DOCKER_BUILD_DONE: Docker image built and containers started
DOCKER_NOT_FOUND: docker not found — install Docker first.
DOCKER_COMPOSE_NOT_FOUND: docker compose not found — install Docker Compose v2.
DOCKER_CRED_CREATED: Created empty ~/.claude/.credentials.json so Docker mounts a file (not a directory). Re-run 'claude auth login' to populate it if using a Claude subscription.
DOCKER_CRED_OK: ~/.claude/ directory found with .credentials.json — will be mounted in bridge container
DOCKER_CRED_NEED_LOGIN: ~/.claude/.credentials.json not found — run 'claude auth login' first
DOCKER_WAIT_URL: ↳ Waiting for proxy public URL (up to 90s)...
DOCKER_URL_TIMEOUT: ↳ Public URL not yet available — register webhook manually after services are ready
DOCKER_WH_MANUAL_HINT: To register the webhook manually (after ngrok starts):
DOCKER_URL_DEBUG: To diagnose (check ngrok startup in container logs):
STEP_TEST_FAIL: Some unit tests failed — check output above. Setup complete but investigate before running.
STEP_HEALTH_FAIL_API: FastAPI not responding — check: pm2 logs 99-api
STEP_HEALTH_FAIL_BRIDGE: Bridge not responding — check: pm2 logs 99-bridge
DONE_PM2: Service status: pm2 status  |  Logs: pm2 logs 99-api
DONE_SYSTEMD: Start services: sudo systemctl start personal-agent personal-agent-bridge
DONE_DOCKER: Containers started — check health: curl http://localhost:8010/health
DONE_MANUAL: Start manually:  cd scripts && backend/venv/bin/uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8010
CAP_DESC:
Select which capabilities to ENABLE.
Unchecked = disabled (smaller image, fewer packages).

Recommended for most users: keep defaults.
Disable Desktop/Browser unless you need GUI automation.
Disable Intent Classifier to save one API call per message.
CAP_FS: File access outside project root  (read files anywhere)
CAP_WIZ_LLM: Wizard AI preview               (LLM call on project create)
MSG_WIZ_TG_INSTALL_NOTICE:
🎉 Services are up!

To finish setup, send `!wizard` to the bot. 5 steps, ~1 min: LLM, capabilities, timezone, then 2 TOTP QR codes.

Type `!cancel` anytime to stop.
TXT_TG_MINIMAL_NOTE: Telegram selected → the remaining settings (LLM/capabilities/timezone/TOTP) will be written with default values automatically. Now we'll only ask for ngrok credentials (Telegram webhook needs a public URL).
MSG_WIZ_WA_NOTIFY: Sending setup notification to WhatsApp — continuing in terminal
CAP_NONINTERACTIVE: Non-interactive mode — applying Basic capabilities preset automatically
AUTH_FAIL_HARD:
Claude auth login failed or was cancelled.
  • Fix the underlying issue and re-run: bash install.sh --docker
  • OR set CLAUDE_AUTH_OPTIONAL=1 to skip (Bridge will not work until you authenticate)
  • OR re-run with API Key instead of Claude Login (in the wizard)
AUTH_EMPTY_CREDS:
Login completed but ~/.claude/.credentials.json is still empty.
  This usually means the OAuth flow was interrupted.  Try one of:
  • Run 'claude auth login' manually and watch for errors
  • Re-run install.sh with API Key authentication instead
  • Set CLAUDE_AUTH_OPTIONAL=1 to skip (Bridge will not work)
AUTH_OPTIONAL_HINT: Tip: set CLAUDE_AUTH_OPTIONAL=1 to skip this check (CI / advanced users)
AUTH_TG_HEADSUP: 🔐 Claude login is starting. You can sign in now.
AUTH_TG_URL_PROMPT:
🔐 *Claude login*

Open the link below in the browser **on the computer where you ran the installer**:

%s

⚠️ Don't open it on your phone — Claude redirects to a localhost callback that the phone can't reach.
AUTH_TG_DONE: ✅ You have signed in to Claude Code. Installation will complete in the terminal.
WIZ_HFS_MSG:
By default the Docker container can only access its own volumes.
Do you want to grant access to all directories on the host computer?
(Only applies in Docker mode — systemd/PM2 already has full access)
WIZ_HFS_READ: Read access — can read any file (e.g. Desktop, Downloads, documents)
WIZ_HFS_WRITE: Write access — can create new files and directories
WIZ_HFS_DELETE: Delete access — can delete files and directories
WIZ_HFS_EDIT: Edit access — can modify existing files
WIZ_HFS_RESULT_RW: Host filesystem: read + write + delete + edit (rw)
